text:
Once upon a time in the lush, green woodlands, there lived an owl named Oliver. Oliver was unlike any owl you'd ever seen—it loved music and had a thing for sock hops hosted by the squirrels who lived in the nearby tree bark groves.

Every night, after a thrilling day discovering the latest folk tunes on the wind-blown leaves, Oliver would embark on an adventure. He'd glide silently through the trees, his feathers shimmering with moonlight, before swooping down into the forest underbrush. There, under the big elm tree that had witnessed countless moons, Oliver would listen to the music his heart drummed out.

One night, Oliver heard something special—a melody he'd never heard before. It was coming from the hollow stump that sat just beneath the old tree's broad branches. Curiosity piqued, he flew down and discovered a hidden clearing. Sitting there was an array of musical instruments, woven from branches and threads gathered from afar.

It was an orchestra
